channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Shadow AI risks deepen as 31% of users get no employer training
item.description: Between one-fifth and one-third of workers use AI outside the influence and governance of the IT function, according to a global survey of 6,000 full-time employees at enterprise organizations. Researchers found a wid...

item.title: Identity is the control plane for distributed infrastructure
item.description: Teleport CEO Ev Kontsevoy makes the case that distributed infrastructure, across cloud, Kubernetes, databases, and servers, can’t be secured by layering more tools on top of fragmented identity systems. He argues for...

item.title: AI traffic is getting bigger, louder, and less predictable
item.description: AI workflows need storage that supports repeated movement across the model lifecycle. Large datasets are ingested, transformed, exported for training, pulled back for evaluation, and refreshed as models evolve. Backbl...

item.title: New infosec products of the month: April 2026
item.description: Here’s a look at the most interesting products from the past month, featuring releases from Advenica, Aptori, Axonius, Broadcom, GlobalSign, Intruder, IP Fabric, Mallory, Secureframe, Siemens, Sitehop, and Virtue AI....

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 1st, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9914, (Fri, May 1st)
That AI Extension Helping You Write Emails? It’s Reading Them First
item.description: Unit 42 uncovers high-risk AI browser extensions. Disguised as productivity tools, they steal data, intercept prompts, and exfiltrate passwords. Protect your browser. The post That AI Extension Helping You Write Email...

item.title: TeamPCP Hits SAP Packages With 'Mini Shai-Hulud' Attack
item.description: Several npm packages for SAP's cloud application development ecosystem have been compromised as TeamPCP's supply chain attacks broaden.

item.title: Another AI-Assisted Software Scan Yields 9-Year-Old Linux Bug
item.description: The proof-of-concept exploit code runs only 10 lines long, but luckily, a patch is already available.

item.title: Anthropic's Mythos Has Landed: Here's What Comes Next for Cyber
item.description: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how the new AI model threatens to completely upend cybersecurity, and what industry leaders are telling the press.

item.title: New Bluekit phishing service includes an AI assistant, 40 templates
item.description: A new phishing kit named Bluekit offers more than 40 templates targeting popular services and includes basic AI features for generating campaign drafts. [...]

item.title: Anthropic Unveils Claude Security to Counter AI-Powered Exploit Surge
item.description: With Mythos signaling a new era of near-instant exploitation, Anthropic positions Claude Security to help defenders keep pace. The post Anthropic Unveils Claude Security to Counter AI-Powered Exploit Surge appeared fi...

item.title: AI Fuels ‘Industrial’ Cybercrime as Time-to-Exploit Shrinks to Hours
item.description: Industrialized cybercrime delivers attacks with greater scale, speed and success. Defenders must match this with use of AI and automation. The post AI Fuels ‘Industrial’ Cybercrime as Time-to-Exploit Shrinks to Hours...

item.title: Remote Code Execution in GitHub Enterprise Server via Git Push Injection (CVE-2026-3854)
item.description: A critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-3854, CVSS 8.7) was disclosed affecting GitHub Enterprise Server and GitHub.com, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary commands on backend servers via a single git push command. Du...

item.title: Copy Fail: New Linux bug enables Root via page‑cache corruption
item.description: Linux flaw CVE‑2026‑31431, ‘Copy Fail,’ lets any local user write four bytes into page cache files, enabling easy escalation to root on major distros. Xint Code researchers warn of a serious Linux flaw, tracked as CVE...

item.title: Great responsibility, without great power
item.description: In this week’s newsletter, Hazel uses International Superhero Day as a springboard to explore why empathy — rather than just technical prowess — is the most essential, underrated superpower for navigating the human si...

item.title: Red Agent and Claude Opus: Securing Production Targets at Scale
item.description: Delivering enterprise-grade continuous AI-powered risk assessment to hundreds of customers through the combined power of Wiz and Anthropic

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-41940 WebPros cPanel & WHM and WP2 (WordPress Squared) Missing Authenticat...

item.title: Agent’s claims on WhatsApp access spark security concerns
item.description: A US agent claimed WhatsApp encryption is fake and Meta can access messages; the probe was abruptly shut, raising security concerns. A US agent claimed WhatsApp encryption is fake, alleging Meta accesses all unencrypt...

item.title: PyTorch Lightning Compromised in PyPI Supply Chain Attack to Steal Credentials
item.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have managed to compromise the popular Python package Lightning to push two malicious versions to conduct credential theft. According to Aikido Security, Sock...

item.title: Three Arrested for Hacking Over 610,000 Roblox Accounts
item.description: Suspects accused of distributing malware and selling access to stolen Roblox accounts on Russian marketplaces

item.title: April KB5083769 Windows 11 update causes backup software failures
item.description: The April 2026 KB5083769 security update breaks third-party backup applications from multiple vendors on systems running Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2. [...]

item.title: Linux Kernel Bug (Copy.Fail) Enables Local Privilege Escalation to Root (CVE-2026-31431)
item.description: A Linux kernel vulnerability, CVE-2026-31431 dubbed Copy Fail, allows an unprivileged local user to gain root by corrupting the page cache of readable files such as /usr/bin/su. The disk file is not modified, so norma...

item.title: Deep#Door Python Backdoor Evades Detection On Windows
item.description: Deep#Door Python RAT uses tunneling and obfuscation to evade detection and steal credentials

item.title: SonicWall Urges Immediate Patching of Firewall Vulnerabilities
item.description: The bugs could be exploited to bypass security controls, access restricted services, and crash firewalls. The post SonicWall Urges Immediate Patching of Firewall Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: SAP NPM Packages Targeted in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: The Mini Shai-Hulud attack introduced a preinstall hook to fetch and execute a Bun binary and bypass security monitoring. The post SAP NPM Packages Targeted in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
item.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other net...

item.title: What Happens in the First 24 Hours After a New Asset Goes Live
item.description: When a new asset goes live, attackers start scanning within minutes. Sprocket Security shows how automated attacks move from discovery to compromise in under 24 hours. [...]

item.title: CISA and Partners Publish Zero Trust Guidance For OT Security
item.description: A new CISA‑led guide explains how zero‑trust security can be applied to operational technology, balancing cyber defence with safety and system availability

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: SMS Blaster Busts, OpenEMR Flaws, 600K Roblox Hacks and 25 More Stories
item.description: The internet is noisy this week. We are seeing some wild new tactics, like people using fake cell towers to send scam texts, while some developers are accidentally downloading tools that peek into their private files...

item.title: New Linux ‘Copy Fail’ flaw gives hackers root on major distros
item.description: An exploit has been published for a local privilege escalation vulnerability dubbed "Copy Fail" that impacts Linux kernels released since 2017, allowing an unprivileged local attacker to gain root permissions. [...]

item.title: Post-quantum encryption for Cloudflare IPsec is generally available
item.description: Cloudflare IPsec now has generally available support for post-quantum encryption via hybrid ML-KEM. We’ve confirmed interoperability with Cisco and Fortinet.

item.title: Oracle Red Bull Racing Team Revs Up Automation to Boost Security
item.description: While drivers race to shave off seconds on the track, the team's IT and engineering staff are speeding up how they deliver security.

item.title: cPanel zero-day exploited for months before patch release (CVE-2026-41940)
item.description: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-41940) in cPanel, a popular web-based control panel for managing web hosting accounts, is being exploited by attackers in the wild. What’s more, attackers didn’...

item.title: UK: Education Sector Faces Surge in Cyber Breaches Despite Stable National Threat Levels
item.description: The British public education sector has faced the nation’s most dramatic increase in cyber breach prevalence over the past year

item.title: The (In)security Landscape of AI-Powered GitHub Actions (Part 2/2)
item.description: When AI meets CI/CD: permission bypasses, prompt injection, and what to do about it.

item.title: Cisco releases open-source toolkit for verifying AI model lineage
item.description: Enterprises pulling models from Hugging Face and other open repositories rarely keep records of how those models are altered after download, leaving organizations with little ability to confirm what they are running i...

item.title: New Python Backdoor Uses Tunneling Service to Steal Browser and Cloud Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a stealthy Python-based backdoor framework called DEEP#DOOR that comes with capabilities to establish persistent access and harvest a wide range of sensitive informa...

item.title: Critical Gemini CLI Flaw Enabled Host Code Execution, Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: An attacker could have planted a malicious configuration to execute commands outside the sandbox. The post Critical Gemini CLI Flaw Enabled Host Code Execution, Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Met Police face criticism for using AI to spy on their own officers
item.description: London police officers have been warned by the Metropolitan Police Federation to watch their backs after the force deployed controversial AI software to investigate misconduct. The staff association, representing more...

item.title: EnOcean SmartServer Flaws Expose Buildings to Remote Hacking
item.description: Claroty researchers discovered two vulnerabilities that can be exploited for security bypass and remote code execution. The post EnOcean SmartServer Flaws Expose Buildings to Remote Hacking appeared first on SecurityW...

item.title: Nine-year-old Linux kernel flaw enables reliable local privilege escalation (CVE-2026-31431)
item.description: Security researchers at Theori have disclosed a high-severity local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431) in the Linux kernel. The flaw, nicknamed “Copy Fail”, has affected virtually every major Li...

item.title: Critical cPanel and WHM bug exploited as a zero-day, PoC now available
item.description: The critical CVE-2026-41940 authentication bypass vulnerability in cPanel, WHM, and WP Squared is being actively exploited in the wild and has been leveraged in attempts since late February. [...]

item.title: EtherRAT Distribution Spoofing Administrative Tools via GitHub Facades
item.description: Intro A sophisticated, high-resilience malicious campaign was identified by Atos Threat Research Center (TRC) in March 2026. This operation specifically targets the high-privilege professional accounts of enterprise a...

item.title: Police dismantles 9 crypto scam centers, arrests 276 suspects
item.description: A joint international operation involving U.S. and Chinese authorities arrested at least 276 suspects and shut down nine cryptocurrency investment fraud centers. [...]

item.title: Critical cPanel & WHM Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day for Months
item.description: The authentication bypass flaw allows attackers to gain administrative access to vulnerable servers. The post Critical cPanel & WHM Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day for Months appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Researchers develop tool to expose GPS signal spoofing in transit networks
item.description: The Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL) has developed a portable detector that identifies GPS spoofing in real time, including during motion, to help protect transportation systems. Spoofing involves transmitting cou...

item.title: Proxmox Backup Server 4.2 arrives with S3 storage support and parallel sync jobs
item.description: Proxmox Backup Server 4.2 is a maintenance and feature update built on Debian 13.4 “Trixie” that adds S3-compatible object storage as a supported backend and introduces parallel processing for sync jobs. The server sh...

item.title: Hackers arrested for stealing and reselling 600,000 Roblox accounts
item.description: Ukrainian police detained three suspects accused of hacking into Roblox accounts and reselling the data on Russian websites, with payments made in cryptocurrency. Police raid (Source: The Prosecutor General’s Office o...

item.title: Automated LLM red teaming gets a learning layer
item.description: Automated red teaming of large language models has settled into a familiar pattern over the past two years. An attacker model generates jailbreak attempts against a target model, an evaluator scores the results, and t...

item.title: Bad bots make up 40% of internet traffic
item.description: The normalization of AI and automation within internet infrastructure is changing how organizations interpret traffic. Activity that once appeared anomalous is now treated as expected behavior. AI agents have emerged...

item.title: Warp open sources its AI terminal client
item.description: Warp, the AI-centric terminal used by close to a million developers, has released the source code for its client on GitHub under the AGPL license, with OpenAI signed on as the founding sponsor of the repository. An ag...

item.title: Fast16 Malware
item.description: Researchers have reverse-engineered a piece of malware named Fast16. It’s almost certainly state-sponsored, probably US in origin, and was deployed against Iran years before Stuxnet: “…the Fast16 malware was designed...

item.title: ‘Copy Fail’ Logic Flaw in Linux Kernel Enables System Takeover
item.description: Affecting the kernel’s authencesn cryptographic template, the vulnerability was introduced in 2017 and impacts all distributions. The post ‘Copy Fail’ Logic Flaw in Linux Kernel Enables System Takeover appeared first...

item.title: Europol Busts Albanian Scam Call Centers in Major Online Fraud Case
item.description: European police arrested 10 suspects after dismantling Albanian scam call centers linked to a €50m ($58m) online investment fraud operation

item.title: New Linux 'Copy Fail' Vulnerability Enables Root Access on Major Distributions
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) flaw that could allow an unprivileged local user to obtain root. The high-severity vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-31431 (...

item.title: Meta accused of violating DSA by failing to safeguard minors
item.description: The European Commission accuses Meta of failing to protect children, allowing users under 13 on Instagram and Facebook, in breach of the DSA rules. The European Commission has accused Meta of violating child safety ru...

item.title: Cyber is the Number One Global “People Risk,” Says Marsh
item.description: Marsh’s 2026 People Risks survey finds cyber‑related challenges dominate, as cyber‑threat literacy tops risks and cyber and AI skills shortages rise

item.title: Sandhills Medical Says Ransomware Breach Affects 170,000
item.description: It took the healthcare organization nearly one year to publicly disclose a data breach after it was targeted by Inc Ransom. The post Sandhills Medical Says Ransomware Breach Affects 170,000 appeared first on SecurityW...

item.title: Large-scale Roblox hacking operation shut down by Ukrainian authorities
item.description: Ukrainian police arrested three hackers who hijacked 610,000 Roblox accounts and sold them for $225,000 in profit. Police in Ukraine arrested three suspects accused of hacking over 610,000 Roblox accounts and selling...

item.title: Google Fixes CVSS 10 Gemini CLI CI RCE and Cursor Flaws Enable Code Execution
item.description: Google has addressed a maximum severity security flaw in Gemini CLI -- the "@google/gemini-cli" npm package and the "google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli" GitHub Actions workflow -- that could have allowed attackers t...

item.title: Identity Access Management Strategy for Non-Human Identities
item.description: Build an identity and access management strategy for non-human identities. Secure service accounts, workloads, and machine identities in the cloud.

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, April 30th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9912, (Thu, Apr 30th)
Danger of Libredtail [Guest Diary], (Wed, Apr 29th)
item.description: [This is a Guest Diary by James Roberts, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu BACS program]

item.title: Agents can now create Cloudflare accounts, buy domains, and deploy
item.description: Starting today, agents can now be Cloudflare customers. They can create a Cloudflare account, start a paid subscription, register a domain, and get back an API token to deploy code right away. Humans can be in the loo...

item.title: Claude Mythos Fears Startle Japan's Financial Services Sector
item.description: Global financial institutions are panicked over Anthropic's new superhacker AI model. Cyber experts aren't quite as worried.

item.title: AI is the present of security
item.description: Security and artificial intelligence have been a winning combination for years. Now, the boom of generative AI has made security accessible to more people.We’ve already covered how AI is a broad discipline that has be...

item.title: Chainguard EKS add-ons are now available in the AWS Marketplace
item.description: Chainguard delivers zero-CVE, FIPS 140-3 EKS add-ons on AWS Marketplace, simplifying secure Kubernetes for regulated environments with full control.

item.title: CVE-2026-31431: “Copy Fail” Linux kernel flaw lets local users gain root in seconds
PCI DSS v4.0.1 Compliance in the cloud and Kubernetes with Sysdig
lightning PyPI Compromise: A Bun-Based Credential Stealer in Python
item.description: A malicious release of the lightning PyPI package ships a credential-stealing Bun payload that runs on import. Snyk has a live advisory. Here's what's in the package, what to rotate, and how the payload pattern connec...

item.title: AARM: Finding a Path to Secure the Agentic Runtime
item.description: Over the past year, I have found myself returning to the same observation in many different conversations: we are not simply watching AI improve. We are watching a new operational layer in computing emerge in front of...

item.title: Five Things we Took Away from Gartner SRM Sydney 2026
item.description: At this year's Gartner Security and Risk Management Summit in Sydney, Rapid7 CISO Brian Castagna joined industry CISO Nigel Hedges for a fireside chat on the decisions security leaders are actually making right now. T...

item.title: Official SAP npm packages compromised to steal credentials
item.description: Multiple official SAP npm packages were compromised in what is believed to be a TeamPCP supply-chain attack to steal credentials and authentication tokens from developers' systems. [...]

item.title: Key Takeaways from the 2026 State of AI in the Cloud Report
item.description: How AI Adoption, Autonomy, and Attacker Innovation Are Reshaping Cloud Security

item.title: Hackers exploit RCE flaws in Qinglong task scheduler for cryptomining
item.description: Hackers are exploiting two authentication bypass vulnerabilities in the Qinglong open-source task scheduling tool to deploy cryptominers on developers' servers. [...]

item.title: CVE-2026-42208: LiteLLM bug exploited 36 hours after its disclosure
item.description: Attackers quickly exploited a critical LiteLLM flaw (CVE-2026-42208) to access and modify sensitive database data via SQL injection. Attackers rapidly exploited a critical vulnerability in LiteLLM Python package, trac...

item.title: Reverse Engineering With AI Unearths High-Severity GitHub Bug
item.description: Wiz used an AI reverse-engineering tool to pinpoint a vulnerability that previously would have been too costly and time-consuming to undertake.

item.title: CVE-2026-41940: cPanel & WHM Authentication Bypass
item.description: Overview On April 28, 2026, cPanel issued a security update to fix a critical vulnerability affecting the cPanel & WHM and WP Squared products. In the cPanel release notes, the bug was described as "an issue with sess...

item.title: AI Finds 38 Security Flaws in Electronic Health Record Platform
item.description: Flaws in OpenEMR's platform — used by more than 100,000 healthcare providers — enabled database compromise, remote code execution, and data theft.

item.title: Designing trust and safety into Amazon Bedrock powered applications
item.description: Generative AI brings promising innovation, transforming how individuals and organizations approach everything from customer service to content creation and more. As AI continues to expand its capabilities, organizatio...

item.title: SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about a new supply chain attack campaign targeting SAP-related npm Packages with credential-stealing malware. According to reports from Aikido Security, SafeDep, Socket...

item.title: cPanel, WHM emergency update fixes critical auth bypass bug
item.description: A critical vulnerability affecting all but the latest versions of cPanel and the WebHost Manager (WHM) dashboard could be exploited to obtain access to the control panel without authentication. [...]

item.title: Vect 2.0 Ransomware Acts as Wiper, Thanks to Design Error
item.description: The emerging ransomware has been deployed against victims of the TeamPCP supply chain attacks, but organizations should think twice before paying for a decryptor.

item.title: A Mini Shai-Hulud Targeting the SAP Ecosystem
item.description: 7 stolen GitHub tokens. 971 repositories. A self-replicating supply chain attack targeting SAP's Node.js packages — and it's still active. Here's what GitGuardian found.

item.title: Supply Chain Campaign Targets SAP npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Malware
item.description: Detect and mitigate malicious npm packages linked to the recent Shai-Hulud-style campaign - Mini Shai Hulud.

item.title: Cursor Extension Flaw Exposes Developer API Keys
item.description: Cursor flaw lets extensions steal API keys and session tokens without user interaction, according to researchers at LayerX

item.title: New Wave of DPRK Attacks Uses AI-Inserted npm Malware, Fake Firms, and RATs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered malicious code in an npm package after a malicious package as a dependency to the project by Anthropic's Claude Opus large language model (LLM). The package in question is "@v...

item.title: European police dismantles €50 million crypto investment fraud ring
item.description: Austrian and Albanian authorities dismantled a criminal ring accused of running a large-scale cryptocurrency investment fraud operation that caused estimated losses of over €50 million ($58.5 million) to victims world...

item.title: Internet censorship index reveals Russia’s lead and widespread content blocking
item.description: Global study shows targeted internet censorship worldwide, with Russia leading; VPNs, news, and adult content are most frequently blocked categories. The Global Internet Censorship Index 2026 offers a clear view of ho...

item.title: Malicious npm Dependency Linked to AI Assisted Commit Targets Crypto Wallets
item.description: Researchers uncover a malicious npm dependency linked to an AI‑assisted code commit that steals sensitive data and exposes crypto wallets

item.title: Wiz Code Week Recap: Securing AI Native Development
item.description: Providing Application Security teams with visibility and guardrails to secure agentic software development and the modern software supply chain

item.title: All supported cPanel versions hit by critical auth bug, now patched
item.description: cPanel fixed a critical authentication flaw that could let attackers access servers. The issue affects all supported versions. cPanel released security updates to address a critical authentication vulnerability that c...

item.title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
item.description: A single third-party OAuth integration can become a direct path into your environment. Push explains how the Vercel breach shows a compromised OAuth app can lead to widespread impact across downstream customers. [...]

item.title: Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure
item.description: The vulnerability allows attackers to read data from a LiteLLM proxy’s database and potentially modify it. The post Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Lotus Wiper Attack Targets Venezuelan Energy Firms, Utilities
item.description: An analysis of the destructive malware reveals sophisticated living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques and detailed strategies for the widespread deletion of data.

item.title: Researchers Track 2.9 Billion Compromised Credentials
item.description: KELA claims infostealers remained the primary access vector for attacks in 2025

item.title: GitHub fixes RCE flaw that gave access to millions of private repos
item.description: In early March, GitHub patched a critical remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-3854) that could have allowed attackers to access millions of private repositories. [...]

item.title: Experts on Experts: The 2026 Threat Landscape is Moving Faster than Defenders Expect
item.description: This week on Experts on Experts, I’m joined by Christiaan Beek, Rapid7’s VP of Threat Analytics, to talk through what we’re seeing in the 2026 threat landscape and how it connects to recent research coming out of Rapi...

item.title: Hundreds of Internet-Facing VNC Servers Expose ICS/OT
item.description: Forescout has identified tens of thousands of exposed RDP and VNC servers that can be mapped to specific industries. The post Hundreds of Internet-Facing VNC Servers Expose ICS/OT appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
item.description: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain. We aren't just talking about AI writing...

item.title: Modern Defensible Architecture: Resilience for the Australian Federal Government
item.description: How Wiz enables Australian government agencies to operationalise MDA with real-time context, zero trust enforcement, and end-to-end cloud visibility.

item.title: What to Look for in an Exposure Management Platform (And What Most of Them Get Wrong)
item.description: Every security team has a version of the same story. The quarter ends with hundreds of vulnerabilities closed. The dashboards are bursting with green. Then someone in a leadership meeting asks: "So, are we actually sa...

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: The hackers exfiltrated the data from Checkmarx’s GitHub environment on March 30, a week after publishing malicious code. The post Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
item.description: cPanel has released security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software. The problem affects all currently su...

item.title: Critical Flaw Turns Vect Ransomware into Data Destroying Wiper
item.description: The Vect 2.0 ransomware wipes large files instead of merely encrypting them, making recovery impossible – even for the attackers

item.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain
item.description: US service members received WhatsApp messages claiming they would be targeted with drones and missiles. The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch Windows flaw exploited as zero-day
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered federal agencies to secure their Windows systems against a vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

item.title: CISA, Microsoft warn of active exploitation of Windows Shell vulnerability (CVE-2026-32202)
item.description: Attackers are exploiting CVE-2026-32202, a zero-click Windows Shell spoofing vulnerability that causes victims’ systems to authenticate the attacker’s server, CISA and Microsoft have warned. About CVE-2026-32202 CVE-2...

item.title: Claude Mythos Has Found 271 Zero-Days in Firefox
item.description: That’s a lot . No, it’s an extraordinary number: Since February, the Firefox team has been working around the clock using frontier AI models to find and fix latent security vulnerabilities in the browser. We wrote pre...

item.title: AI-powered honeypots: Turning the tables on malicious AI agents
item.description: Just as AI brings time-saving advantages to our lives, it brings similar advantages to threat actors. We can take the advantage back. This blog shows how generative AI can be used to rapidly deploy adaptive honeypot s...

item.title: 38 Vulnerabilities Found in OpenEMR Medical Software
item.description: Some of the vulnerabilities discovered by Aisle can be exploited to access and alter sensitive patient information. The post 38 Vulnerabilities Found in OpenEMR Medical Software appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Chrome 147, Firefox 150 Security Updates Rolling Out
item.description: The browser refreshes resolve critical and high-severity vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution. The post Chrome 147, Firefox 150 Security Updates Rolling Out appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited ConnectWise and Windows Flaws to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, base...

item.title: Microchip expands Trust Shield with PQC-ready root of trust and secure boot controllers
item.description: Microchip Technology is expanding its portfolio of Trust Shield, PQC‑ready devices with the TS1800 Platform Root of Trust controller and the TS50x secure boot controller. The devices are designed to help system archit...

item.title: Kaseya agentic IT management unifies data and automates ticketing, security and backups
item.description: Kaseya has introduced an agentic IT management platform powered by Kaseya Intelligence, combining unified data across IT operations, cybersecurity, and resilience with an execution layer that autonomously triages tick...

item.title: DigitalOcean AI-Native Cloud unifies infrastructure, inference, and agents for production AI
item.description: DigitalOcean has introduced the AI-Native Cloud, an end-to-end platform built for the inference and agentic era. Spanning infrastructure, core cloud, inference, data, and managed agents, it already supports production...

item.title: amazeeClaw simplifies production deployment of AI agents with regional control
item.description: amazee.ai has announced the launch of amazeeClaw, a managed OpenClaw hosting platform that enables developers and enterprises to deploy production-ready AI agents with data sovereignty and regional control without hav...

item.title: U.S. CISA adds Microsoft Windows Shell and ConnectWise ScreenConnect flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds Windows Shell and ConnectWise ScreenConnect flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Ag...

item.title: Virtue AI PolicyGuard turns AI policies into enforceable runtime guardrails
item.description: Virtue AI has announced PolicyGuard, a system that enables enterprises to define, edit, and enforce custom AI runtime protection guardrails across models, agents, and applications. Most organizations have “AI acceptab...

item.title: ShinyHunters exploit Anodot incident to target Vimeo
item.description: The video platform Vimeo confirmed a security breach via Anodot that exposed metadata, video titles, and some user emails. Vimeo said some user data was accessed after a breach at Anodot. Anodot is a company that prov...
